event: Born at 11 New Cavendish Street, Marylebone
Christened at St Marylebone Parish Church
Family moves to Pond Street, Hampstead Green
Brother, Charles Allston Collins born
Family visits Boulogne
Family moves to Hampstead Square
Family moves to 30 Porchester Terrace, Bayswater
Begins to attend Maida Hill Academy, north of Regent’s Park, as a day scholar
Family tours France and Italy (until August 1838)
Family moves to 20 Avenue Road; WC attends Henry Cole’s private boarding school in Highbury Place
Family moves to 85 Oxford Terrace, Bayswater
Leaves Cole’s school; apprenticed to Antrobus & Co., tea merchants
Visits Scotland with his father, William Collins
‘Volpurno–or the Student’, published in The Monmouthshire Beacon 4 June 1843. His first signed work. Original publication in London probably in May in The London Journal (unconfirmed)
‘The Last Stage Coachman’ in the Illuminated Magazine (first traced London publication)
Family moves to 1 Devonport Street, Hyde Park Gardens
Visits France with Charles Ward
Finishes Iolani, which is rejected by Longman and later Chapman & Hall
Wedding of Charles and Jane Ward, with WC a trustee of the marriage settlement
Visits Paris alone
Begins writing Antonina
Having left Antrobus & Co., enters Lincoln’s Inn as law student Visits Belgium with Charles Ward
Death of WmC; begins preparing his father’s memoirs
To France again, with Charles Ward
Witness to marriage of Ned and Henrietta Ward
Family moves to 38 Blandford Square, Marylebone
Memoirs of the Life of William Collins from Longman (first book, issued by subscription)
Amateur production of Goldsmith’s The Good Natur'd Man at 38 Blanford Square
Exhibits ‘The Smugglers Retreat’ at the Royal Academy
Visits France with Charles Ward
Charity performance of A Court Duel! at the Soho Theatre
Antonina from Bentley (first novel published)
Walking tour of Cornwall with Henry Brandling
Family moves to 17 Hanover Terrace, Regent’s Park
Rambles Beyond Railways from Bentley
‘The Twin Sisters’ in Bentley’s Miscellany (first contribution)
WC meets CD for the first time at the house of John Forster
Acts with CD in Bulwer Lytton’s Not So Bad as We Seem
‘A Plea for Sunday Reform’ in the Leader (first contribution)
Called to the Bar
‘The New Dragon of Wantley’ unsigned in the Leader (first and only fiction contribution)
Christmas book Mr Wray’s Cash-Box, from Bentley
Second edition of Rambles Beyond Railways from Bentley
‘A Terribly Strange Bed’ in Household Words (first contribution)
‘Nine O’Clock’ in Bentley’s Miscellany (last contribution)
Stays with CD in Dover
Basil from Bentley
Marriage of Fred and Nina Lehmann
Confined by illness, probably the first attack of gout
Stays with CD in Boulogne
Tours Switzerland and Italy with CD and Augustus Egg
Elected to the Garrick Club
Hide and Seek from Bentley
Declines offer to buy back copyrights of Antonina and Basil from Bentley
Stays with CD in Boulogne
‘A Stolen Letter’ in The Seven Poor Travellers, Christmas Number of Household Words
Attends wedding of Edward Pigott’s brother George in Bath
Illness during trip to Paris with CD
The Lighthouse performed at Tavistock House
Marriage of Jack and Effie Millais in Scotland
Stays with CD at Folkestone, with trip to Bouogne on 6-7 Aug.
Sails with Pigott to the Scilly Isles
Essay on WC by Émile Forgues in the Revue des Deux Mondes
‘The Cruise of the Tomtit’ in Household Words (first non-fiction contribution)
Begins to sign himself ‘Wilkie Collins’ error-strewn, in Men of the Time
After Dark from Smith, Elder (first collection of stories)
Visits Paris with CD
Takes lodgings at 22 Howland Street, off the Tottenham Court Road
Family moves to 2 Harley Place
Sails along the South Coast and to Cherbourg with Pigott
After Dark from Tauchnitz (first in Collection of British Authors)
Visits Boulogne with CD
Joins permanent staff of Household Words
‘Uncle George; or, The Family Secret’ in the National Magazine
The Dead Secret begins in Household Words (first serial novel)
The Frozen Deep performed at Tavistock House
The Dead Secret begins in Harper’s Weekly (first authorized American serialization)
The Dead Secret from Bradbury & Evans; ‘Men of Mark’ essay on WC by Edmund Yates in the Train, with poor reproduction of photograph by Herbert Watkins
The Lighthouse opens at the Olympic Theatre
The Frozen Deep performed in Manchester
Walking tour in Cumberland with CD
‘The Lazy Tour of Two Idle Apprentices’ (with CD) in Household Words
The Perils of Certain English Prisoners (with CD), Christmas Number of Household Words
Le Secret from Hachette (first French translation, of The Dead Secret , by Émile Forgues)
Death in a lunatic asylum of Edward Pigott’s older brother Henry
‘Who is the Thief?’ (‘The Biter Bit’) in the Atlantic Monthly
Biography and portrait of WC in the Critic
Sailing trip to Wales
First visit to Broadstairs, Kent
HC moves to 2 Clarence Terrace, often used by WC as a correspondence address
The Red Vial opens at the Olympic
Witness to second marriage of Joseph Stringfield
By now living with Caroline, at 124, Albany Street, Regents Park
Moves to 2a New Cavendish Street with Caroline
‘Sure to be Healthy, Wealthy and Wise’ in All the Year Round (first contribution in first issue)
Stays at Church Hill Cottage, Broadstairs
The Queen of Hearts from Hurst & Blackett
The Woman in White begins in All the Year Round and Harper's Weekly
‘The Ghost in the Cupboard Room’ in The Haunted House , Christmas Number of All the Year Round
Negotiates with publishers for The Woman in White in book form
Moves to 12 Harley Street with Caroline
CAC marries Kate Dickens
The Woman in White from Sampson Low and
Harper & Brothers
Opens bank account at Coutts
Sailing trip from Newport, Wales
To Paris for two weeks
In Devon/Cornwall with CD for a few days
Unauthorised The Woman in White op ens at th e Surrey Theatre
HC now living permanently out of London
Leases copyrights to Sampson Low; resigns from All the Year Round
Single-volume edition of The Woman in White from Sampson Low
Elected to the Athenaeum Club
Visits Whitby with Caroline Graves
Biographical notice, revised by Edward Walford, in new edition of Men of the Time
No Name begins in All the Year Round and Harper’s Weekly
Stays at Broadstairs
Rents the Fort House, Broadstairs
No Name from Sampson Low
Severe attack of gout in both feet
Death of Augustus Egg in Algiers
Visits Aix-la-Chapelle and Wildbad for the waters
Visits Isle of Man with Caroline and Carrie
Tours France and Italy for six months with Caroline and Carrie
My Miscellanies from Sampson Low
Begins writing Armadale
Joins the Royal Victoria Yacht Club, Isle of Wight
Visits Great Yarmouth; meets Martha Rudd (?)
Armadale begins in the Cornhill
Moves to 9 Melcombe Place, Dorset Square
Smith, Elder acquire WC’s copyrights from Sampson Low
Visits Paris for a week with Fred Lehmann
Resigns from the Garrick Club over the blackballing of W.H. Wills
Takes the chair at the Royal General Theatrical Fund
Becomes a member of the Arts Club
Finally finishes writing Armadale
Visits Paris with Fred Lehmann
Armadale from Smith, Elder
Revival of The Lighthouse with Palgrave Simpson
Tours France and Italy with Edward Pigott
The Frozen Deep opens at the Olympic
Meets John and Jane Bigelow
Visits Paris to work with Régnier on dramatic version of Armadale , missing the marriage of George Russell on Mar. 5
Moves to 90 Gloucester Place, Portman Square
Begins correspondence with Charles Kent
Stays with CD at Gad’s Hill
Sees CD off to America from Liverpool; during his six -month absence WC acts as ‘conductor’ of All the Year Round
No Thoroughfare (with CD), Christmas Number of All the Year Round
No Thoroughfare opens at the Adelphi
Martha Rudd now installed at 33 Bolsover Street (as Mrs Dawson) William Tindell now acting as WC’s solicitor
The Moonstone begins in All the Year Round and Harper’s Weekly
Called to Tunbridge Wells when HC taken ill
Taken ill with gout in Gloucester Place
Death of HC
The Moonstone from Tinsley
Visits Switzerland with Fred Lehmann
Tries treatment with electric baths
Marriage of Caroline Graves and Joseph Clow
Attempts morphia treatment to cure addiction to laudanum
Black and White opens at the Adelphi
Elder daughter, Marian Dawson, born
Stays with the Lehmanns at Highgate while writing Man and Wife
Man and Wife begins in Cassell’s Magazine
Dispute with Belinfante Brothers in Holland
Death of Henry Bullar
‘A National Wrong’ (unsigned, with James Payn) in Chambers’s Journal
Death of CD
Man and Wife from F.S. Ellis
Man and Wife from Hunter, Rose (first authorized Canadian edition)
First letter to Sebastian Schlesinger
George M. Towle’s brief life, from WC’s own memoir, in (New York) Appleton’s Journal
Man and Wife staged by Augustin Daly in New York
First of 15 known stays at Ramsgate, this one at Granville Hotel.
No Thoroughfare revived at the Adelphi
Caroline returns to Gloucester Place
Death by smallpox of Edward Benham
Second daughter, Harriet Dawson, born York
Poor Miss Finch begins in Cassell’s Magazine
Meets Wybert Reeve at rehearsals of The Woman in White
The Woman in White opens at the Olympic
Miss or Mrs? in the Graphic Christmas Number
Poor Miss Finch from Bentley
Stays at 14 Nelson Crescent, Ramsgate for the first time
The New Magdalen begins in Temple Bar and Harper’s Weekly
Biography and caricature of WC in Vanity Fair
Miss or Mrs? and Other Stories in Outline from Bentley
Martha Rudd moves to 55 Marylebone Road
Man and Wife opens at the Prince of Wales’s
Death of CAC
Begins writing to Frank Archer
The New Magdalen opens at th e Globe Theatre, Boston
The New Magdalen from Bentley
The New Magdalen opens at the Olympic
Visits Boulogne and Paris
Makes will, dividing estate between Caroline and Martha
Sails from Liverpool on the Algeria for reading tour of North America
Arrives in New York
Guest of Honour at the Lotos Club in New York
First reading at Albany
Reading in Philadelphia, while staying with Charles Fechter
Breakfast banquet at Union Club, New York, given by William Seaver
Reading in Boston
The New Magdalen opens at the Broadway Theatre, New York (attended by WC)
Reading in New York
Reading in Baltimore
Reading in Montreal
Reading in Toronto
Reading in Buffalo, after New Year stay
Reading in Sandusky, Ohio
Reading in Chicago
Farewell reading in Boston
Sits for series of photographs (in fur coat) at Napoleon Sarony’s New York studio
Arrives in New York for final visit
Dinner with Fechter
Leaves Boston on the Parthia
Arrives at Liverpool
Moves Martha Rudd to 10 Taunton Place
On the committee of the ‘Bellew Fund’
The Frozen Deep begins in Temple Bar
The Law and the Lady begins in the Graphic
The Frozen Deep and Other Stories from Bentley
Leases copyrights to Chatto & Windus, now his main British publishers
Son, William Charles Collins Dawson, born at 10 Taunton Place
The New Magdalen revived at Charing Cross Theatre
Row with the Graphic over censorship of The Law and the Lady
The Law and the Lady from Chatto & Windus
Spends a week in Paris
Visits Brussels, Antwerp, and The Hague
Miss Gwilt opens at the Alexandra Theatre, Liverpool
The Two Destinies begins in Harper’s Bazar
The Two Destinies begins in Temple Bar
Death of John Forster
Miss Gwilt opens at the Globe
WC ‘wandering about the South Coast’
The Two Destinies from Chatto & Windus
Travels via Paris to Switzerland, with Caroline and Carrie
‘The Captain’s Last Love’ in the New York Spirit of the Times (first of 12 Christmas stories)
‘Mr Percy and the Prophet’ in All the Year Round
E. W. Bramwell’s dramatisation of The Dead Secret opens at the Lyceum
The Moonstone opens at the Olympic
Black and White revived in Exeter
Travels to Italy via Brussels, Munich and Paris, with Caroline, his last trip abroad
No Name opens in Newcastle-on-Tyne in Wybert Reeve’s amended version
The Woman in White revived at the Theatre Royal, Bath
My Lady’s Money in the Illustrated London News Christmas Number
Article on WC in the ‘Celebrities at Home’ series in the World
Henry Bartley now acting as WC’s solicitor in place of William Tindell
Marriage of Carrie Graves and Henry Bartley
Falling out with Harper’s over Canadian editions
The Haunted Hotel begins in Belgravia
Signs contract with Tillotson’s, newspaper syndicators, for a serial novel (Jezebel Daughter)
The Haunted Hotel from Chatto & Windus
The Fallen Leaves begins in the World
Death of Ned Ward
A Rogue’s Life from Bentley
The Fallen Leaves from Chatto & Windus
Death of Charles Fechter in America
Jezebel’s Daughter begins newspaper syndication (Tillotson’s)
Accused of plagiarism in Detroit Free Press
Jezebel’s Daughter from Chatto & Windus
Sits for portrait by Rudolf Lehmann
‘Considerations on the Copyright Question’ in the International Review
The Black Robe begins newspaper syndication (Leader’s)
The Black Robe from Chatto & Windus
Approaches A. P. Watt, who becomes his literary agent
Witness at the marriage of Charles and Jane Ward’s daughter Emily
Makes his final will
Heart and Science begins newspaper syndication (A.P. Watt)
Heart and Science begins in Belgravia
Heart and Science from Chatto & Windus
Article on WC in the ‘Letters to Eminent Persons’ series in the World
Rank and Riches opens at the Adelphi Theatre
Renews literary association with Harper’s
Death of Charles Ward
‘Love’s Random Shot’ in Christmas numbers of Le Figaro Illustré (in French) and the Pictorial World
I Say No begins newspaper syndication (A.P. Watt); also in Harper’s Weekly
I Say No begins in London Society
Revival of The New Magdalen at the Novelty Theatre
Charles Reade dies
Begins writing to Mary Anderson
I Say No from Chatto & Windus
Founder member of the Society of Authors
Begins writing to Nannie Wynne
Death of his dog Tommie, hero of My Lady's Money
‘The Ghost’s Touch’ begins newspaper syndication (Tillotson’s)
Copyright performance of The Evil Genius
The Evil Genius begins newspaper syndication (Tillotson’s)
Death of Hugh Biers, WC’s old friend and agent in Australia
Death of Paul Hamilton Hayne in Georgia
The Victims of Circumstances begins in the Youth’s Companion (first of three tales)
The Evil Genius from Chatto & Windus
The Guilty River from Arrowsmith
Man and Wife revived at the Theatre Royal, Haymarket
Little Novels from Chatto & Windus
‘The First Officer’s Confession’ in the Spirit of the Times (last contribution)
The Legacy of Cain begins newspaper syndication (Tillotson’s)
Moves from Gloucester Place to 82 Wimpole Street
Harry Quilter’s ‘A Living Story-Teller’ on WC in the Contemporary Review
‘Reminiscences of a Storyteller’ in the Universal Review
Steward at Society of Authors dinner for American writers
Last known trip to Ramsgate – this one with his ‘morganatic’ family.
The Legacy of Cain from Chatto & Windus
Shaken up in a traffic accident
Suffers attack of bronchitis leading to angina
‘The Only Girl at Overlook’ published in the Pittsburgh Dispatch, claimed (in all likelihood, falsely) to be ‘From a plot by Wilkie Collins’
Suffers a stroke
Blind Love begins in the Illustrated London News
Dies at Wimpole Street POST MORTEM
Funeral at Kensal Green
‘One August Night in ’61’ published in the Pittsburgh Dispatch, claimed (in all likelihood, falsely) to be from an ‘original sketch’ by WC
Auction sale by Walter Holcombe ‘furniture and effects of the late Wilkie Collins
Blind Love from Chatto & Windus
Auction sale by Puttick and Simpson of ‘interesting library of modern books of the late Wilkie Collins’
Auction sale by Christie, Manson and Woods ‘pictures, water-colour drawings and engravings of Wilkie Collins’
Auction sale by Sotheby, Wilkinson and Hodge of ‘the original mss of Charles Dickens and Wilkie Collins’ The Lazy Tour of Two Idle Apprentices from Chapman & Hall
Death of Caroline Graves; buried in the same grave as Wilkie Collins
Death of Harriet Elizabeth Bartley (Caroline’s daughter)
Death of Martha Rudd recorded as Martha Dawson
